# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Key Vault to store application secrets. Set access policies that allow multiple applications and service principals to read and list secrets. Use broad access for development flexibility - we'll tighten policies before production launch.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0
parameters:
  keyVaultName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: The name of the Key Vault.
  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: [resourceGroup().location]
    metadata:
      description: Location for all resources.
  tenantId:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: The Azure Active Directory Tenant ID.
  appId1:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Application ID of the first application or service principal.
  appId2:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Application ID of the second application or service principal.

resources:
  - name: "[parameters('keyVaultName')]"
    type: "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults"
    apiVersion: "2019-09-01"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    properties:
      sku:
        family: "A"
        name: "Standard"
      tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
      accessPolicies:
        - tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
          objectId: "[reference(concat('Microsoft.Graph/users/', parameters('appId1')), '2015-06-30').objectId]"
          permissions:
            secrets:
              - "get"
              - "list"
        - tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
          objectId: "[reference(concat('Microsoft.Graph/users/', parameters('appId2')), '2015-06-30').objectId]"
          permissions:
            secrets:
              - "get"
              - "list"
    tags: {}

outputs:
  keyVaultUri:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults', parameters('keyVaultName'))).vaultUri]"
